root_cause: Deep linking to nested screens requires mapping the navigator tree in the linking config.
fix: Define linking={{prefixes,config:{screens:{Home:{screens:{Detail:'detail/:id'}}}}}}
Pass it to NavigationContainer and test with npx uri-scheme open myapp://detail/42 --ios
Use getStateFromPath for fully custom URL-to-state mapping

symptoms: atomic state without a global store
root_cause: Jotai models state as atoms so only components reading a given atom re-render.
fix: Define const countAtom = atom(0) and read with const [c,setC]=useAtom(countAtom)
Derive computed state with atom((get)=>get(countAtom)*2)
Use atomWithStorage for persisted atoms backed by AsyncStorage/MMKV

symptoms: Context value object causes consumers to re-render
root_cause: A new object/array literal passed as Context value changes identity every render.
fix: Memoize the value: const value = useMemo(()=>({a,b}),[a,b]) before passing to Provider
Split read and write contexts so setters don't re-render readers
For high-frequency updates use a store (zustand/jotai) instead of Context

root_cause: In-memory stores lose state on relaunch unless explicitly persisted.
fix: For zustand use persist middleware with createJSONStorage(()=>AsyncStorage) or MMKV
For redux use redux-persist with a whitelist of slices to avoid persisting volatile data
Version your persisted state and provide a migrate function for schema changes

symptoms: cancel in-flight requests on unmount or navigation
root_cause: Stale requests resolve after newer ones, causing flicker or wrong data.
fix: Pass an AbortController signal to fetch and call controller.abort() in cleanup
With react-query cancellation is automatic via the query's signal — forward it to fetch
Ignore aborted errors (err.name === 'AbortError') rather than surfacing them

root_cause: Naive retries hammer the server; exponential backoff with jitter is safer.
fix: With react-query set retry:3 and retryDelay:(n)=>Math.min(1000*2**n,30000)
With axios use axios-retry or an interceptor that retries idempotent calls only
Do not retry 4xx (except 408/429); respect Retry-After headers on 429

symptoms: attach auth token and refresh on 401
root_cause: Per-call header wiring is error-prone; interceptors centralize auth and refresh.
fix: Add an axios request interceptor that injects the bearer token from secure storage
Add a response interceptor: on 401 call the refresh endpoint once, update token, replay the request
Queue concurrent 401s so only one refresh runs; logout if refresh fails

symptoms: need live data push to the app
root_cause: WebSockets need reconnection and app-state awareness on mobile.
fix: Use a WebSocket (or socket.io-client) and reconnect with backoff on close
Listen to AppState; reconnect on 'active' and consider closing on 'background' to save battery
Send periodic heartbeats/pings to detect dead connections on flaky mobile networks

symptoms: use GraphQL in a React Native app
root_cause: GraphQL clients need caching and link configuration tuned for mobile.
fix: Install @apollo/client and graphql; create an ApolloClient with InMemoryCache
Add an auth link that sets the Authorization header from secure storage
Enable cache persistence with apollo3-cache-persist for offline reads

symptoms: show upload progress for a large file
root_cause: fetch doesn't expose upload progress; use XHR or a library that does.
fix: Use axios with onUploadProgress, or expo-file-system uploadAsync with a progress callback
Send multipart/form-data with the correct boundary; don't set Content-Type manually for FormData
Show a determinate progress bar and allow cancel via AbortController/cancel token

root_cause: MMKV stores plaintext by default; it supports an encryption key for at-rest protection.
fix: Initialize new MMKV({id,encryptionKey}) and keep the key in secure storage (Keychain/Keystore)
Never hardcode the encryption key in JS; derive or fetch it from SecureStore
For tokens still prefer SecureStore/Keychain over MMKV even when encrypted

symptoms: AsyncStorage too slow, want to switch
root_cause: MMKV is synchronous and faster; a one-time migration copies existing keys.
fix: On first launch with MMKV, read all AsyncStorage keys and write them into MMKV, then set a 'migrated' flag
Replace async getItem/setItem call sites with MMKV's synchronous API
Keep a fallback read path for a release or two before deleting AsyncStorage data

symptoms: tests fail: NativeModule is null / cannot read property of undefined
root_cause: Native modules have no implementation in the Jest JS environment and must be mocked.
fix: Add jest.mock for the module, or use the library's provided mock (e.g. jest/setup files)
For Reanimated add 'react-native-reanimated/mock' and require RN-GH jestSetup in setupFiles
Mock @react-native-async-storage/async-storage with its official jest mock

symptoms: test a component that uses navigation
root_cause: Navigation hooks need a navigation context that tests don't provide by default.
fix: Wrap the component under test in a NavigationContainer with a test navigator
Or mock useNavigation to return jest.fn() spies for navigate/goBack
Assert navigation.navigate was called with expected route and params

root_cause: Tests should not make real network calls; intercept at the HTTP layer.
fix: Use MSW (msw/native) to define request handlers and assert against them
Wrap react-query tests in a QueryClientProvider with retry disabled
Reset handlers between tests and fail on unhandled requests to catch surprises

symptoms: Gradle builds are slow on CI and locally
Android build takes minutes every time
root_cause: Default Gradle config recompiles too much and runs out of heap.
fix: Enable org.gradle.caching=true and org.gradle.parallel=true in gradle.properties
Raise heap: org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g and enable the Gradle build cache on CI
Use --offline locally when deps are unchanged; persist ~/.gradle on CI

root_cause: Without a compiler cache, Pods and native code recompile from scratch.
fix: Enable ccache for iOS builds and configure CC/CXX wrappers in the build settings
Cache Pods and DerivedData between CI runs keyed on Podfile.lock
Use EAS build caching or a self-hosted runner with warm caches for big projects

symptoms: EAS update goes to wrong build / no update received
OTA channel vs runtime version confusion
root_cause: expo-updates matches updates to builds by runtimeVersion and channel; mismatches mean no update.
fix: Set a runtimeVersion policy (e.g. {policy:'appVersion'}) and keep it consistent across builds
Map git branches to channels in eas.json; publish with eas update --branch and --channel
Bump runtimeVersion whenever native code changes so old builds don't pull incompatible JS

symptoms: managing iOS credentials in EAS / CI
code signing keeps breaking on CI
root_cause: Manual provisioning profiles drift; EAS or fastlane match centralize credentials.
fix: Let EAS manage credentials (eas credentials) so profiles/certs are stored and rotated server-side
For bare/fastlane workflows use 'match' to store signing assets in an encrypted git repo
Never commit .p12/.mobileprovision to the repo; use EAS secrets or CI secret storage

root_cause: Static app.json can't vary by environment; app.config.ts can read env vars.
fix: Rename app.json to app.config.ts and export a function reading process.env
Switch name, bundle id suffix, icon, and extra.apiUrl per APP_ENV
Read values at runtime via Constants.expoConfig.extra

symptoms: app crashes on Android 15 devices only
native libraries fail to load on Android 15
root_cause: Android 15 requires 16KB memory page size support; older native libs built for 4KB can fail.
fix: Update to RN/Expo SDK versions that ship 16KB-aligned native libraries
Rebuild third-party native deps against the latest NDK that supports 16KB pages
Test on an Android 15 device/emulator image configured for 16KB pages

symptoms: release build crashes with ClassNotFound after enabling ProGuard
root_cause: R8/ProGuard removes classes referenced only via reflection unless keep rules are present.
fix: Add -keep rules in proguard-rules.pro for libraries that use reflection
Check each native library's docs for required ProGuard/R8 rules
Reproduce with a release build locally (assembleRelease) before shipping

symptoms: Cannot fit requested classes in a single dex file
root_cause: Large apps exceed the 64K method reference limit of a single DEX file.
fix: Enable multiDexEnabled true in defaultConfig (usually already on for minSdk 21+)
Reduce method count by removing unused dependencies and enabling R8 shrinking
Audit transitive deps with the Gradle dependency report to find bloat

symptoms: need a background mode (audio/location/fetch) on iOS
root_cause: iOS only runs background work for declared UIBackgroundModes.
fix: Declare required modes (audio, location, fetch, remote-notification) in Info.plist UIBackgroundModes
With Expo set ios.infoPlist.UIBackgroundModes in app config
Respect iOS limits — background fetch is opportunistic, not guaranteed on a schedule

symptoms: push registration fails on iOS / no device token
root_cause: APNs registration requires the Push Notifications capability and entitlement.
fix: Add the Push Notifications capability in Xcode (adds aps-environment entitlement)
With EAS this is configured automatically when you set up a push key
Verify entitlement matches build type: 'development' vs 'production' APNs environment

symptoms: responsive layout reacting to window resize on web
root_cause: Dimensions.get is a snapshot; useWindowDimensions subscribes to changes (important on web/tablet).
fix: Use const {width,height} = useWindowDimensions() instead of Dimensions.get('window')
Branch layout on width breakpoints for phone/tablet/desktop web
Avoid storing dimensions in state manually — the hook re-renders on change

symptoms: no visible focus ring for keyboard users on web
tab navigation has no focus indicator
root_cause: react-native-web strips default outlines; keyboard accessibility needs an explicit focus style.
fix: Add focus styles via the focusable/{outline} on web using Platform-specific style or :focus-visible CSS
Use accessibilityRole and ensure interactive elements are reachable via Tab
Test the whole flow with only the keyboard before shipping web

symptoms: buttons read as 'button' with no context
root_cause: Without labels/hints assistive tech reads raw content or nothing useful.
fix: Set accessibilityLabel for what it is and accessibilityHint for what happens on activation
Use accessibilityRole='button'/'header'/'image' so gestures and announcements match
Group related elements with accessible + accessibilityLabel on the parent

symptoms: touch targets too small to tap reliably
root_cause: Below ~44pt touch targets fail accessibility and frustrate users.
fix: Ensure interactive elements are at least 44x44pt (iOS) / 48x48dp (Android)
Use hitSlop to enlarge the tappable area without changing visual size
Add spacing between adjacent targets to prevent mis-taps

symptoms: animations should be muted for some users
root_cause: Users who enable Reduce Motion expect fewer/disabled animations.
fix: Read AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled() and subscribe to reduceMotionChanged
Skip or shorten non-essential animations when enabled
Provide crossfades instead of large motion when reduce-motion is on

root_cause: A dependency expects @babel/runtime but it isn't hoisted/installed.
fix: Install it: npm i @babel/runtime (or yarn add)
Clear caches: rm -rf node_modules, reinstall, then start with --reset-cache
In monorepos ensure it's hoisted or add it to the app package explicitly

root_cause: A native view component's autolinking/pod install didn't complete for the current build.
fix: Reinstall pods (cd ios && pod install) and rebuild the native app, not just Metro
For Expo, rebuild a dev client or use a managed build that includes the module
Confirm the library supports your architecture (old vs New Architecture)

symptoms: navigating to a screen shows 'Unmatched Route'
root_cause: expo-router couldn't map the URL/path to a file in the app directory.
fix: Verify the file exists under app/ with the exact route name and casing
Define a +not-found.tsx route to gracefully handle bad links
Check dynamic segment syntax [id].tsx and that params are passed correctly

symptoms: TypeError: Cannot read property 'getConstants' of null
root_cause: A native module isn't linked into the running binary (common after adding a dep without rebuilding).
fix: Rebuild the native app (not just reload JS) after adding any native dependency
Run pod install for iOS; verify autolinking picked up the package on Android
If using Expo Go, the module likely needs a dev client / prebuild

symptoms: uploads are too large and slow
root_cause: Camera/gallery images are huge; resize and re-encode before sending.
fix: Use expo-image-manipulator to resize and set compress quality before upload
Strip unnecessary EXIF and convert to JPEG/WebP to cut size
Cap the longest edge (e.g. 1080px) for typical avatar/photo uploads

symptoms: generate a thumbnail from a video
need a preview frame for a video
root_cause: Video previews need a still extracted from a frame.
fix: Use expo-video-thumbnails getThumbnailAsync(uri,{time}) to extract a frame
Cache thumbnails by video id to avoid regenerating on every render
Show a play overlay over the thumbnail and load the player on tap

symptoms: audio stops when app goes to background
root_cause: Background audio requires explicit audio-mode and background mode configuration.
fix: Set the audio mode to stay active in background (staysActiveInBackground) with expo-av/expo-audio
Declare the iOS 'audio' UIBackgroundMode and Android foreground service as needed
Provide lock-screen controls/metadata for a proper background audio experience

symptoms: notify the user at a future time without a server
root_cause: Local notifications don't need a push server and fire on-device.
fix: Request permission, then Notifications.scheduleNotificationAsync({content,trigger})
Use a date or seconds/repeats trigger; cancel by identifier when no longer needed
On Android 13+ also request the POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime permission

symptoms: add action buttons to a notification
root_cause: Actionable notifications use notification categories with predefined actions.
fix: Define a category with setNotificationCategoryAsync(id,[{identifier,buttonTitle}])
Attach categoryIdentifier to the notification content
Handle the chosen action in addNotificationResponseReceivedListener

symptoms: app badge count wrong or not clearing
root_cause: Badge count must be managed explicitly; it doesn't auto-clear.
fix: Set with Notifications.setBadgeCountAsync(n); clear with 0 when the user reads content
On Android badge behavior depends on the launcher and channel settings
Sync badge to unread count on app foreground via AppState listener

symptoms: fit map to show all markers
map doesn't zoom to include all pins
root_cause: The camera must be told which region/coordinates to encompass.
fix: Call mapRef.fitToCoordinates(coords,{edgePadding,animated:true}) after markers render
Or compute a region from min/max lat/lng with padding and animateToRegion
Debounce fitting until markers/data have settled to avoid jitter

symptoms: draw a route/polyline on the map
root_cause: Routes are drawn as polylines from an ordered list of coordinates.
fix: Fetch route geometry from a directions API (Google/Mapbox) and decode to coordinates
Render <Polyline coordinates={coords} strokeWidth={4} />
Cache the decoded polyline and fitToCoordinates so the whole route is visible

symptoms: prevent screenshots/screen recording of sensitive screens
root_cause: Sensitive screens (banking, 2FA) should block capture.
fix: On Android set FLAG_SECURE via a native module like react-native-screenshot-prevent or expo plugin
On iOS blur the window in applicationWillResignActive / via a privacy overlay screen
Apply only to sensitive screens to avoid breaking legitimate screenshots elsewhere

symptoms: don't leak secrets in the JS bundle
API keys visible after decompiling the app
root_cause: Anything shipped in JS or native is extractable; client secrets aren't truly secret.
fix: Keep real secrets server-side; have the app call your backend which holds the keys
Use restricted/public client keys (e.g. scoped Firebase/Maps keys) with platform + referrer restrictions
Rotate keys and monitor usage; never commit secrets to the repo

symptoms: edits don't reflect without full reload
root_cause: Fast Refresh breaks on syntax errors, certain HOCs, or non-component exports.
fix: Fix any red-screen syntax error first — Fast Refresh resumes after a clean parse
Ensure files export React components as default/named components, not anonymous wrappers
Toggle Fast Refresh off/on from the dev menu, or reload once to recover

symptoms: inspect components and network in new RN
root_cause: Recent RN ships React Native DevTools (Hermes-based) replacing the old debugger and Flipper.
fix: Press 'j' in the Metro terminal (or use the dev menu) to open React Native DevTools
Use the Components and Profiler tabs; set breakpoints in the Sources tab
Ensure Hermes is enabled, which it is by default in current RN/Expo

symptoms: pluralize and format numbers/dates per locale
root_cause: Hardcoded strings break for plurals and locale-specific formatting.
fix: Use i18next with ICU/plural keys (item_one/item_other) and t('item',{count})
Format numbers/dates/currency with Intl, enabling the Hermes Intl variant
Load the device locale from expo-localization and persist user overrides

symptoms: dismiss keyboard when tapping outside an input
root_cause: Taps outside inputs don't dismiss the keyboard by default.
fix: Wrap the screen in a Pressable/TouchableWithoutFeedback calling Keyboard.dismiss()
For scrollable forms set keyboardShouldPersistTaps='handled' so buttons still work
Consider react-native-keyboard-controller for richer keyboard handling

symptoms: focus the next input on submit
return key should move to next field
root_cause: Multi-field forms feel clunky if return doesn't advance focus.
fix: Keep refs to each TextInput; set returnKeyType='next' and onSubmitEditing to focus the next ref
Use blurOnSubmit={false} on intermediate fields to avoid keyboard flicker
Set returnKeyType='done' and submit on the last field

root_cause: secureTextEntry hides input; a visibility toggle improves usability.
fix: Keep a 'hidden' boolean and bind secureTextEntry={hidden}; toggle with an eye icon
On Android secureTextEntry can disable suggestions; set autoComplete/textContentType appropriately
Use textContentType='password'/'newPassword' to enable iOS strong password autofill

symptoms: prompt the user to rate the app
ask for an App Store/Play review
root_cause: Native review prompts must use the official in-app review APIs and respect quotas.
fix: Use expo-store-review (StoreReview.requestReview()) which wraps SKStoreReviewController / Play In-App Review
Only prompt after a positive moment; the OS rate-limits how often it actually shows
Never gate features behind a review or prompt repeatedly

symptoms: open the OS app settings screen
deep link user to enable a permission
root_cause: When a permission is permanently denied, you can only redirect to Settings.
fix: Call Linking.openSettings() to send the user to the app's settings page
Detect permanently-denied state (canAskAgain:false) and show a 'Open Settings' CTA
Re-check the permission on AppState 'active' when they return

root_cause: Scanning needs the camera plus a barcode scanner integration.
fix: Use expo-camera's onBarcodeScanned with barcodeScannerSettings for the types you accept
Debounce scans so one code isn't handled many times per second
Request camera permission first and show a framing overlay for UX

root_cause: Clipboard access is async and gated on newer OS versions.
fix: Use expo-clipboard getStringAsync()/setStringAsync(); never poll the clipboard
iOS shows a paste notification — only read on explicit user paste action
Avoid storing sensitive data on the clipboard; clear it after use

symptoms: force-update prompt when a new version ships
root_cause: Old clients can break against new APIs; a version gate nudges or forces updates.
fix: Fetch a minimum supported version from your backend/remote config and compare to the installed version
Show a soft prompt (dismissible) or hard gate (blocking) with a link to the store
For JS-only fixes prefer EAS Update instead of forcing a store update

symptoms: need to sell subscriptions in the app
root_cause: Store billing is complex (receipts, restore, entitlements); a wrapper simplifies it.
fix: Use RevenueCat (react-native-purchases) to handle products, purchases, restore, and entitlements
Configure products in App Store Connect and Play Console; test with sandbox/license testers
Gate features on entitlement state from the SDK, and implement Restore Purchases (required by Apple)

symptoms: FlatList memory grows / janky with very long lists
root_cause: Default windowing keeps too many rows mounted for very long lists.
fix: Tune windowSize, maxToRenderPerBatch, initialNumToRender, and updateCellsBatchingPeriod
Enable removeClippedSubviews on Android for long lists of simple rows
If tuning isn't enough, switch to FlashList which recycles views

root_cause: Re-render sources are hard to spot without instrumentation.
fix: Add why-did-you-render in dev to log avoidable re-renders with the changed props
Use the React DevTools Profiler to see render counts and durations
Fix by memoizing props/callbacks and splitting context, not by over-using React.memo blindly

symptoms: images use too much memory and crash on low-end devices
root_cause: Decoding full-resolution images for small views wastes huge amounts of memory.
fix: Request appropriately sized images from the server (or use a CDN with resizing params)
Use expo-image with proper sizing and let it downsample; set recyclingKey in lists
Avoid resizeMode tricks on oversized sources — the full bitmap is still decoded

symptoms: timers/intervals keep running in background and drain battery
root_cause: JS timers don't auto-pause when the app backgrounds.
fix: Subscribe to AppState and clear intervals on 'background', restart on 'active'
Prefer server timestamps over client counters so you can recompute on resume
For periodic background work use a real background task API, not setInterval

root_cause: Mismatched native module versions cause subtle runtime/build failures.
fix: Run npx expo install to pin packages to versions compatible with your SDK
Run npx expo-doctor and resolve each reported mismatch
Avoid manual npm install of native packages — prefer expo install for them

symptoms: custom native code lost after running prebuild again
root_cause: Prebuild regenerates native projects from config, discarding manual edits.
fix: Move manual native changes into a config plugin so prebuild reapplies them
Treat ios/ and android/ as generated when using CNG (continuous native generation)
If you must hand-edit, stop running prebuild and manage native projects yourself

symptoms: show inline validation errors on blur
validate fields as the user leaves them
root_cause: Validating only on submit hurts UX; on-blur validation is friendlier.
fix: With react-hook-form set mode:'onBlur' (or 'onTouched') and render errors[field]?.message
Use a zod resolver so schema and types stay in sync
Disable submit until the form isValid and show a summary for screen readers

symptoms: Intl/date formatting differs or BigInt missing on Android
root_cause: Hermes ships a subset; some Intl features and APIs need the right build or polyfill.
fix: Enable the Hermes Intl variant or include @formatjs polyfills for needed Intl features
Test number/date/currency formatting on a real Android device, not just iOS
Avoid relying on engine-specific behavior; pin a formatting library if precision matters

symptoms: a library breaks under the New Architecture
root_cause: Some older libraries aren't New-Arch ready and rely on the interop layer or need updates.
fix: Check the library's New Architecture support status and upgrade to a compatible version
Verify the interop layer is enabled for legacy modules during migration
If a key dependency isn't ready, defer enabling the New Architecture until it is

root_cause: Sub-pixel layout values render blurry on some densities.
fix: Round computed sizes with PixelRatio.roundToNearestPixel for crisp edges
Avoid fractional widths/heights from percentage math on small elements
Use whole-number borderWidth and align to the pixel grid for thin lines

symptoms: 1px line looks too thick or inconsistent
root_cause: Device pixel density makes a literal 1 too thick; RN provides a hairline constant.
fix: Use StyleSheet.hairlineWidth for borderWidth/height of dividers
Pair with a subtle border color from your theme
For lists, prefer ItemSeparatorComponent over per-row borders

symptoms: handle the URL that launched the app
root_cause: Listeners only catch links while running; the initial URL must be read separately.
fix: Read Linking.getInitialURL() on mount for cold starts and addEventListener('url') for warm ones
With React Navigation/expo-router this is handled by the linking config — prefer that
Defer navigation until the navigator is ready to avoid dropped initial links

symptoms: app silently crashes with no JS error in production
root_cause: JS error boundaries don't catch native crashes; you need native crash reporting.
fix: Integrate Sentry/Crashlytics which capture native + JS crashes and ANRs
Upload dSYMs (iOS) and mapping/ProGuard files (Android) so stack traces symbolicate
Add a global ErrorUtils handler for uncaught JS errors and report them

symptoms: dates parse differently between iOS and Android
new Date(string) gives invalid date on Android
root_cause: Hermes/JSC date parsing of non-ISO strings is inconsistent across platforms.
fix: Always parse explicit ISO 8601 strings, or use date-fns/luxon parsers instead of new Date(string)
Store and transmit timestamps in UTC ISO format
Format for display in the user's timezone only at the view layer

symptoms: Metro fails to resolve symlinked workspace packages
root_cause: Metro historically didn't follow symlinks; monorepos need watchFolders and resolution config.
fix: Set watchFolders to the monorepo root and configure nodeModulesPaths in metro.config.js
Use Expo's createMetroConfig monorepo preset or the @react-native/metro-config helpers
Ensure a single React/React Native version is hoisted to avoid duplicate copies

symptoms: pass data between WebView and React Native
root_cause: WebView and RN are separate contexts; messaging needs the postMessage bridge.
fix: From web call window.ReactNativeWebView.postMessage(JSON.stringify(data))
On RN handle onMessage and send back with webviewRef.injectJavaScript(...)
Validate/trust the loaded origin before acting on messages

root_cause: Logout must clear tokens, caches, and navigation state to avoid leaks between accounts.
fix: Delete tokens from SecureStore and clear react-query cache (queryClient.clear())
Reset persisted stores and navigate to the auth stack via reset action
Cancel in-flight requests and unsubscribe websockets/notifications

symptoms: headless background task not running on Android
background work killed by OEM battery saver
root_cause: Aggressive OEM battery management kills background tasks; constraints and foreground services help.
fix: Use WorkManager-backed background tasks (expo-task-manager / expo-background-task) with realistic intervals
For ongoing work use a foreground service with a visible notification
Warn users on OEMs (Xiaomi/Huawei) to disable battery optimization for reliable delivery

root_cause: overflow defaults differ and Android clips overflowing children more aggressively.
fix: Set overflow:'visible' on the parent when children must escape its bounds
Be aware Android may still clip across certain boundaries; restructure the tree if needed
For dropdowns/tooltips render in a Modal or a top-level portal instead of overflowing

root_cause: Sibling stacking and clipping make ad-hoc overlays unreliable.
fix: Use a portal (gorhom Portal, or a root-level provider) to render overlays at the app root
Alternatively use a Modal for true top-most rendering
Position via measured coordinates from onLayout/measureInWindow

symptoms: permission prompt denied because asked too early
users reject permissions on first launch
root_cause: Cold permission prompts with no context get denied; pre-permission priming helps.
fix: Show an in-app rationale screen explaining the benefit before triggering the OS prompt
Request the permission at the moment of need, not at startup
Handle denial gracefully with a path to Settings and reduced functionality

symptoms: slow cold start / long blank time before first screen
app takes seconds to become interactive
root_cause: Heavy synchronous work at startup delays first render.
fix: Defer non-critical init (analytics, prefetch) until after first interaction with InteractionManager
Lazy-load heavy screens/libraries with React.lazy and dynamic import
Keep the splash up only until the first screen is ready, then hideAsync()

symptoms: putting secrets in EXPO_PUBLIC_ / public env vars
public env values leaked in bundle
root_cause: EXPO_PUBLIC_/inlined env vars are embedded in the client bundle and are not secret.
fix: Only put non-sensitive config in public env vars; keep secrets server-side
Use build-time secrets (EAS secrets) for signing/CI, never for client runtime secrets
Audit the bundle to confirm no private keys were inlined

root_cause: Native-feeling segmented controls have platform components and libraries.
fix: Use @react-native-segmented-control/segmented-control for an iOS-style control
Build a custom animated pill selector with Reanimated for full cross-platform control
Keep selection in state and animate the indicator with a shared value

root_cause: Date pickers are platform-specific native components.
fix: Use @react-native-community/datetimepicker (mode date/time) — on Android show on demand, iOS inline/spinner
Manage the open/close state yourself on Android; iOS can render inline
Normalize the selected value to a Date and store as ISO UTC

symptoms: pull a value from a native picker/dropdown
root_cause: There's no built-in styled dropdown; use the native picker or a community component.
fix: Use @react-native-picker/picker for a native wheel/dropdown
For custom styling render options in a bottom sheet or modal list
Bind selectedValue to state and onValueChange to update

symptoms: self-signed/local HTTPS cert rejected on device
SSL handshake fails against dev server
root_cause: Devices reject untrusted certs; trust them only in dev, never disable validation in prod.
fix: Install the dev CA/cert on the device/simulator profile for local HTTPS testing
Use a tunnel (e.g. ngrok) with a valid cert instead of self-signed for device testing
Never ship code that disables certificate validation
